title: Telehealth Addiction Treatment
description: Fully telehealth treatment for opioid and alcohol use disorders, delivered through a secure mobile app. Includes medication (Suboxone/buprenorphine), clinical care, peer recovery coaching, and case management. Harm-reduction based — no abstinence requirement, and patients are never discharged for returning to use.
engage: Get started at boulder.care/get-started or check insurance eligibility at start.boulder.care/eligibility. Care is delivered through the Boulder Care mobile app.
content:
Services

  • Medication for Addiction (MAT): Prescription buprenorphine/naloxone (Suboxone) and other medications for opioid and alcohol use disorder, managed by licensed addiction medicine clinicians.
  • Clinical Care: Diagnosis and treatment of substance use disorders and co-occurring conditions like anxiety, depression, PTSD, and insomnia.
  • Peer Recovery Support: Coaching from Peer Recovery Specialists with lived experience in long-term recovery.
  • Case Management: Help with social services, employment support, and safe-housing referrals.
  • Care Navigation: Coordination of resources and support between visits.

How It Works

All care is delivered remotely through a secure mobile app — there are no in-person visits required. Boulder Care uses a harm-reduction approach with no abstinence requirement and states they "never discharge you for missed visits, returning to use, or using other substances."

Who It's For

Adults with opioid or alcohol use disorder. Available to Oregon residents (and many other states).

Cost

Accepts dozens of public and private insurance plans, including Medicaid, Medicare, UnitedHealthcare, Molina, Cigna, and Aetna. Check eligibility online before enrolling.
